summary: Confirm RP2350-PiZero pinout (HDMI, SD, USB host)
description: RESOLVED from the Waveshare schematic + official demo source (docs/RP2350-PiZero-schematic.pdf, .ref/demo). RP2350B, 520KB SRAM, 16MB flash, PSRAM pad UNPOPULATED. HDMI TMDS on GPIO32-39 (D2=32/33, D1=34/35, D0=36/37, CLK=38/39). microSD SPI: SCK=30, MOSI=31, MISO=40, CS=43, card-detect=22, ~12.5MHz. PIO-USB host: D+=28, D-=29. I2C0 SDA=6/SCL=7, UART0 TX=0/RX=1, WS2812 LED=2. Full pinout in README.md.

summary: Decision: libdvi (PIO) DVI, 320x240 fb hardware-doubled to 640x480
description: RESOLVED. HDMI is wired to GPIO32-39, but HSTX is hardwired to GPIO12-19 only, so HSTX CANNOT drive this board's HDMI -- libdvi (Wren6991/PicoDVI, PIO) is mandatory, matching Waveshare's reference demo. Framebuffer = 320x240 RGB565 (~154KB, fits 520KB SRAM); libdvi scans out pixel/line-doubled (DVI_VERTICAL_REPEAT=2) to 640x480p 60Hz. CoCo 256x192 centers in 320x240 -> 512x384 with blank borders on screen. Config = pico_sock_cfg, invert_diffpairs=false, pio_set_gpio_base(pio,16). See README.md.

summary: DEFERRED: reconcile DVI (~252MHz) vs PIO-USB (120/240MHz) system clock
description: DEFERRED to the USB phase (PIZERO-11+). Decision: prove XRoar rendering first by running DVI at its native ~252MHz TMDS clock (close to the AMOLED port's 250MHz, so emulation gets full headroom and no clock compromise). The conflict only matters once USB host is added: libdvi 640x480p60 needs sys ~252MHz, Pico-PIO-USB needs exactly 120/240MHz (252/48 is fractional -> USB jitter). A custom DVI driver would NOT help -- the bit-rate=sysclk coupling is inherent to PIO DVI. When revisited: feed libdvi a 240MHz-friendly 640x480 timing (24MHz pixel clock ~57Hz, monitor-tolerated) so USB is happy, and verify on a real monitor.

summary: Fork reusable skeleton from the AMOLED port
description: Copy board-agnostic code from ~/github/coco-rp2350-waveshare-touch-amoled-18: lib/xroar_core, lib/coco_machine, src/coco_boot.cpp/h, AUTORUN.md, deploy.sh. OMIT board-specific: lib/sh8601, lib/qspi_pio, lib/hal (touch/IMU), src/hw_config.c (AMOLED SD pins), and the AMOLED-rotation parts of the blitter. src/main.cpp copied as a starting structure but display calls will be rewritten for libdvi.

summary: PlatformIO config for RP2350B + earlephilhower + libdvi
description: platformio.ini: RP2350B board id (-DPICO_RP2350B=1), framework=arduino (earlephilhower). Vendor libdvi (Wren6991/PicoDVI, from .ref/demo Arduino/01-DVI) into lib/; lib_deps Pico-PIO-USB + Adafruit TinyUSB, carlk3/no-OS-FatFS-SD. USB Stack = Adafruit TinyUSB. Keep -O2, build_unflags=-Os, lib_ldf_mode=deep+. Arduino.h before XRoar headers (_Bool gotcha). System clock per PIZERO-02b.

summary: libdvi bring-up: 320x240 test pattern on HDMI monitor
description: Port the hello_dvi reference (libdvi, pico_sock_cfg, TMDS on GPIO32-39, gpio_base=16, vreg ~1.2V) to show a static 320x240 RGB565 test pattern scaled to 640x480p60 on a real HDMI monitor. DVI IRQ/scanout on core1. Confirm stable sync and correct RGB565 colors (watch byte order). Baseline before integrating emulation.

coco_machine currently leans on PSRAM for the 64KB CoCo RAM. PiZero PSRAM pad is unpopulated; allocate CoCo RAM from main SRAM (static/SRAM malloc) like the AMOLED port did. Verify against the SRAM budget alongside the 154KB framebuffer + libdvi TMDS buffers.

Re-derive the blitter for the 320x240 RGB565 framebuffer: map the nibble-packed VDG 256x192 buffer + palette into a centered 256x192 region (32px L/R, 24px T/B border), no rotation (landscape native). Feed scanlines to the libdvi colour-buffer queue. Re-derive any lookup tables. Simpler than the AMOLED portrait-rotation math.

summary: SD mount + ROM load on PiZero SD pins
description: Wire no-OS-FatFS-SD to the PiZero microSD SPI pins (SCK=30, MOSI=31, MISO=40, CS=43, CD=22, ~12.5MHz). Mount the card and load /coco/bas12.rom + /coco/extbas11.rom into the 16KB ROM image via the existing coco_boot ROM loader.

summary: Milestone: Color BASIC boots to OK on HDMI
description: Integrate emulator loop + blitter + libdvi scanout: XRoar runs, VDG output renders, Color BASIC reaches the OK prompt on the HDMI monitor. Parity with the AMOLED port's Phase 2.

Get the AUTORUN.TXT autotype + disk/cart demo path working on HDMI (parity with AMOLED Phase 3): @DISK/@CART/@DIRECT directives, LOADM .bin direct-load, autotype queue. Confirm a self-running BASIC/demo on the monitor.

summary: PIO-USB host bring-up: enumerate a USB keyboard
description: Bring up Pico-PIO-USB + Adafruit TinyUSB host on the PIO-USB port (D+=28, D-=29), reusing the device_info demo pattern (.ref/demo Arduino/02-USB) and ~/github/waveshare-rp2350-usb-a. Host stack runs on core1. Enumerate a USB keyboard and log HID reports over serial. Depends on the clock decision in PIZERO-02b (USB needs 120/240MHz).

summary: Map USB HID keycodes -> CoCo keyboard matrix
description: Translate USB HID keyboard reports to CoCo DSCAN codes and inject via the existing coco_machine_press_key() matrix hook. Handle modifiers/shift and key-hold/debounce (the AMOLED port held keys a few frames). Replaces serial/autotype as the primary input.
Map a USB HID gamepad to the CoCo joystick axes/buttons through the XRoar joystick path. Stretch goal after keyboard works.

summary: Dual-core split: emu on core0, video/USB/SD on core1
description: core0 = CoCo emulation; core1 = libdvi DVI scanout (IRQ-driven) + SD/USB host servicing. Pico-PIO-USB host already wants to run on core1, and libdvi scanout runs on core1 too -- confirm they coexist (PIO blocks: libdvi on pio0, PIO-USB on another). Depends on the system-clock outcome from PIZERO-02b. Target >=30fps per the README.

summary: Performance pass: instrument timing, tune clock/vreg
description: Add per-frame timing instrumentation (CPU/blit/scanout) like the AMOLED port. Tune the system clock and vreg within the PIZERO-02b constraints to hit >=30fps. Compare real-time % against the AMOLED port baseline (~36%).
